articleTitle: Oral and topical peptides for skin aging: systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials.
takeaways: Based on systematic review evidence, both oral and topical peptide treatments demonstrated measurable improvements in skin hydration, elasticity, wrinkle reduction, and brightness compared to placebo controls
Peptide-based therapies work by enhancing collagen synthesis and improving extracellular matrix integrity, targeting the underlying mechanisms of skin aging rather than just surface symptoms

The meta-analysis found peptide treatments to be safe with minimal adverse effects reported across randomized controlled trials, making them a viable option for addressing multiple signs of skin aging simultaneously
Both delivery methods (oral supplements and topical applications) showed efficacy, giving consumers flexibility in choosing peptide-based interventions based on personal preference and convenience
